title: Seed a worked design doc example in Phase 1 before readers write one
description: 01-talking-to-agents/03-context-is-everything.md:67 introduces design docs as central to the methodology. 01-talking-to-agents/02-the-art-of-intent.md:159 and 01-talking-to-agents/03-context-is-everything.md:109 both ask the reader to write design-doc-style prompts before the reader has seen a full example. The first real design doc example is in 01-talking-to-agents/05-first-build.md:32-65. 04-proving-it/01-on-your-own.md:33 later says 'You have seen enough design docs at this point' but a reader who did not build extras has seen exactly one. Fix: Either move the design doc example from 05-first-build.md earlier into 03-context-is-everything.md as a concrete template the reader can imitate, or add a short 'worked design doc' sidebar in 02-the-art-of-intent.md. Also add a second example for a Rust CLI project somewhere in Phase 2 so the reader sees a non-web example before the capstone.
